text: In this case, I present a forty-two-year-old female, gravita two, para two, with a history of abnormal uterine bleeding, pelvic pain, and a right complex ovarian mass measuring eight by six centimeters. She has a past surgical history of a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, cesarean section times two, and a bilateral tubal ligation. Procedure performed was a robotic assisted total laparoscopic hysterectomy, bilateral salpingectomy, right oophorectomy, incidental appendectomy secondary to adhesions, and water cystoscopy.
The surgeon uses a vaso-sealing device to secure the meso-appendix, effectively sealing the appendiceal artery and skeletonizing the appendix to the cecum.

The surgeon uses an Echelon 45mm flexible stapler with a blue load to perform an appendectomy through the vaginal orifice following a hysterectomy. The appendix is removed due to adhesions to the right ovarian complex mass, which resulted in an endometrioma.

The surgeon uses a grasper to deliver the appendix through the vaginal orifice. The ileocecal junction is visually inspected for patency and hemostasis, confirming successful robotic appendectomy via the vaginal approach.
